content.content:
{"body":"## Summary
- Remove `swift-testing` SPM dependency from `Package.swift` — the `Testing` framework is built into Swift 6.0 / Xcode 16+ toolchain
- Drops transitive `swift-syntax` dependency (faster resolves, smaller dependency graph)

## Why
Dependabot PR #20 bumped `swift-testing` to 6.2.4, which fails CI with `missing required module '_TestingInternals'` on the `macos-15` runner. Rather than pinning to an older version, removing the redundant dependency is the correct fix.

## Test plan
- [x] `swift build` passes locally
- [x] `swift test` — all 79 tests pass (they use `import Testing` which resolves to the built-in framework)
- [ ] CI passes on this PR","headRefName":"chore/remove-swift-testing-spm-dep","title":"chore: remove swift-testing SPM dependency"}

content.text: Now I can see the issue clearly. `swift build` succeeds but `swift test` fails with `_TestingInternals` errors. The CI is restoring a **stale SPM cache** (with old `swift-testing` artifacts) via the `restore-keys: spm-macOS-` prefix match. Let me check the CI workflow and local files.

content.content:
1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→  workflow_dispatch:
     9→
    10→permissions:
    11→  contents: read
    12→
    13→concurrency:
    14→  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
    15→  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
    16→
    17→jobs:
    18→  changes:
    19→    name: Detect changes
    20→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    21→    permissions:
    22→      contents: read
    23→      pull-requests: read
    24→    outputs:
    25→      swift: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.swift }}
    26→      rust: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.rust }}
    27→      sample-app: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.sample-app }}
    28→    steps:
    29→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
    30→      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3
    31→        id: filter
    32→        with:
    33→          filters: |
    34→            swift:
    35→              - 'macOS/**'
    36→              - 'Package.swift'
    37→              - 'Package.resolved'
    38→            rust:
    39→              - 'windows/**'
    40→              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'
    41→            sample-app:
    42→              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/**'
    43→              - '!Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'
    44→
    45→  swift-lint:
    46→    name: Swift Lint
    47→    needs: changes
    48→    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    49→    runs-on: macos-15
    50→    steps:
    51→      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
    52→
    53→      - name: Install SwiftLint
    54→        run: |
    55→          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
    56→          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
    57→          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      swift: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.swift }}
      rust: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.rust }}
      sample-app: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.sample-app }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            swift:
              - 'macOS/**'
              - 'Package.swift'
              - 'Package.resolved'
            rust:
              - 'windows/**'
              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'
            sample-app:
              - 'Examples/windows/SampleApp/**'
              - '!Examples/windows/SampleApp/src-tauri/**'

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.59.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config macOS/.swiftlint.yml macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config macOS/.swiftformat macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

  swift-test:
    name: Swift Build & Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5
        with:
          path: |
            .build
            ~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm
          key: spm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: spm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Build
        run: swift build

      - name: Test
        run: swift test

  rust-lint:
    name: Rust Lint (Linux)
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy, rustfmt

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2
        with:
          workspaces: "windows -> windows/target"

      - name: Check formatting
        working-directory: windows
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Clippy (core — platform-agnostic)
        run: >
          cargo clippy --manifest-path windows/Cargo.toml
          -p audio-capture-core
          -- -D warnings -W clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks

  rust-clippy-windows:
    name: Rust Clippy (Windows)
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2
        with:
          workspaces: "windows -> windows/target"

      - name: Clippy (windows crate)
        run: >
          cargo clippy --manifest-path windows/Cargo.toml
          -p audio-capture-windows
          -- -D warnings

  rust-test:
    name: Rust Test (${{ matrix.package }})
    needs: changes
    # No job-level if: matrix jobs must always run so each expanded name
    # (e.g. "Rust Test (audio-capture-core)") is individually reported to
    # GitHub. A job-level if causes GitHub to report only the unexpanded
    # template name, which never satisfies the required status checks.
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - package: audio-capture-core
            os: ubuntu-latest
          - package: audio-capture-windows
            os: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2
        if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
        with:
          workspaces: "windows -> windows/target"

      - name: Test
        if: needs.changes.outputs.rust == 'true'
        run: cargo test --manifest-path windows/Cargo.toml -p ${{ matrix.package }}

  sample-app-lint:
    name: Sample App Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.sample-app == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    defaults:
      run:
        working-directory: Examples/windows/SampleApp
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: Examples/windows/SampleApp/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: TypeScript type check
        run: npx tsc --noEmit

      - name: npm audit
        run: npm audit --audit-level=high

  dependency-review:
    name: Dependency Review
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
      - uses: actions/dependency-review-action@2031cfc080254a8a887f58cffee85186f0e49e48 # v4
        with:
          fail-on-severity: high
          deny-licenses: GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0

content.text:
Now I see the full picture. The `swift build` succeeds but `swift test` fails with `missing required module '_TestingInternals'`. The CI cache (`.build` directory) is **poisoned** — it was restored from an old cache containing `swift-testing` artifacts, saved after the first failed run, and now every subsequent run gets the same bad cache.

Let me check out the PR branch and fix this.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      swift: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.swift }}
      csharp: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.csharp }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@fbd0ab8f3e69293af611ebaee6363fc25e6d187d # v4.0.1
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            swift:
              - 'macOS/**'
              - 'Package.swift'
              - 'Package.resolved'
            csharp:
              - 'csharp/**'
              - 'Examples/windows/**'

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.59.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config macOS/.swiftlint.yml macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config macOS/.swiftformat macOS/Sources/ macOS/Tests/

  swift-test:
    name: Swift Build & Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.swift == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7 # v5
        with:
          path: |
            .build
            ~/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm
          key: spm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: spm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Resolve packages
        run: swift package resolve

      - name: Build
        run: swift build

      - name: Test
        run: swift test

  csharp-build-test:
    name: C# Build & Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.csharp == 'true'
    runs-on: windows-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@87b7050bc53ea08284295505d98d2aa94301e852 # v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: 10.0.x

      - name: Restore
        run: dotnet restore csharp/AudioCapture.Tests/AudioCapture.Tests.csproj

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build csharp/AudioCapture.Tests/AudioCapture.Tests.csproj -c Release --no-restore

      - name: Test
        run: dotnet test csharp/AudioCapture.Tests/AudioCapture.Tests.csproj -c Release --no-build --verbosity normal

  csharp-format:
    name: C# Format Check
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.csharp == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@87b7050bc53ea08284295505d98d2aa94301e852 # v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: 10.0.x

      - name: Check formatting
        run: dotnet format csharp/AudioCapture/AudioCapture.csproj --verify-no-changes --verbosity diagnostic
        env:
          EnableWindowsTargeting: true

  dependency-review:
    name: Dependency Review
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
      - uses: actions/dependency-review-action@2031cfc080254a8a887f58cffee85186f0e49e48 # v4
        with:
          fail-on-severity: high
          deny-licenses: GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0

content.text:
Good, I can see the PR branch state. The `swift-testing` removal from `Package.swift` looks correct. The CI failure is due to a **poisoned cache** — the `.build` directory in the cache has stale `swift-testing` artifacts that conflict with the toolchain's built-in `Testing` framework.

Let me first verify the build works locally, then fix the CI.

1→# AudioCaptureKit
     2→
     3→[![OpenSSF Scorecard](https://api.securityscorecards.dev/projects/github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/badge)](https://scorecard.dev/viewer/?uri=github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit)
     4→[![OpenSSF Best Practices](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007/badge)](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007)
     5→
     6→Cross-platform audio capture library — microphone + system audio mixed into a single stereo recording with AES-256-GCM encryption.
     7→
     8→## Platform Support
     9→
    10→| Platform | Language | Audio API | Directory |
    11→|----------|----------|-----------|-----------|
    12→| macOS 14+ | Swift 6 | Core Audio Taps + AVFoundation | `macOS/` |
    13→| Windows 10+ | Rust 1.75+ | WASAPI | `windows/` |
    14→
    15→## Shared Architecture
    16→
    17→Both platforms follow the same **Capture → Processing → Storage** pipeline:
    18→
    19→```
    20→┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
    21→│  Mic Capture     │────▶│              │     │                  │
    22→│  (mono)          │     │  StereoMixer │────▶│  EncryptedFile   │
    23→│                  │     │              │     │  Writer (.wav)   │
    24→│  System Capture  │────▶│              │     │                  │
    25→│  (stereo)        │     └──────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
    26→└─────────────────┘
    27→```
    28→
    29→**Stereo mixing** is identical on both platforms: Left = mic + system L, Right = mic + system R. Mic audio is mono, mixed into center of the stereo field. System audio preserves its natural stereo image.
    30→
    31→**Encryption**: Both platforms use AES-256-GCM to stream-encrypt audio chunks as they're captured — no plaintext on disk.
    32→
    33→## macOS
    34→
    35→### Requirements
    36→
    37→- macOS 14.0+ (system audio capture requires macOS 14.2+)
    38→- Swift 6.0+
    39→- Entitlement: `com.apple.security.device.audio-input`
    40→- TCC permission: "Screen & System Audio Recording" (for system audio)
    41→
    42→### Installation (Swift Package Manager)
    43→
    44→```swift
    45→dependencies: [
    46→    .package(path: "../AudioCaptureKit")  // local
    47→    // or
    48→    // .package(url: "https://github.com/yourorg/AudioCaptureKit.git", from: "1.0.0")
    49→]
    50→```
    51→
    52→### Quick Start
    53→
    54→```swift
    55→import AudioCaptureKit
    56→
    57→let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    58→    sampleRate: 48000,
    59→    bitDepth: 16,
    60→    channels: 2,
    61→    outputDirectory: URL.temporaryDirectory
    62→)
    63→let session = CompositeCaptureSession(configuration: config)
    64→session.delegate = myDelegate
    65→
    66→try session.configure(config)
    67→try await session.startCapture()
    68→
    69→let result = try await session.stopCapture()
    70→print("Recorded \(result.duration)s to \(result.fileURL)")
    71→```
    72→
    73→### Testing
    74→
    75→```bash
    76→swift test
    77→```
    78→
    79→See `macOS/` for source code and `Examples/macOS/` for the sample app.
    80→
    81→## Windows
    82→
    83→### Requirements
    84→
    85→- Windows 10+
    86→- Rust 1.75+
    87→
    88→### Building
    89→
    90→```bash
    91→cd windows
    92→cargo build --release
    93→```
    94→
    95→### Crate Structure
    96→
    97→| Crate | Purpose |
    98→|-------|---------|
    99→| `audio-capture-core` | Platform-agnostic models, traits, stereo mixer, encrypted writer |
   100→| `audio-capture-windows` | WASAPI mic capture, WASAPI loopback (system audio), device enumeration |
   101→
   102→### Quick Start
   103→
   104→```rust
   105→use audio_capture_core::session::CompositeSession;
   106→use audio_capture_core::models::CaptureConfig;
   107→use audio_capture_windows::{WasapiMic, WasapiLoopback};
   108→
   109→let config = CaptureConfig::new(48000, 16, 2);
   110→let session = CompositeSession::new(config);
   111→
   112→session.start()?;
   113→// ...
   114→let result = session.stop()?;
   115→```
   116→
   117→See `windows/` for source code.
   118→
   119→### Sample App (Tauri v2)
   120→
   121→A desktop GUI at `Examples/windows/SampleApp/` built with Tauri v2 (Rust backend + React frontend). Records mic + system audio, shows real-time level meters, lists recordings, and supports device selection and encryption toggle.
   122→
   123→**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Rust 1.77+, Windows 10 SDK
   124→
   125→```bash
   126→cd Examples/windows/SampleApp
   127→npm install
   128→npm run tauri dev      # Dev mode with hot reload
   129→npm run tauri build    # Produces .exe + MSI installer in src-tauri/target/release/bundle/
   130→```

1→# Security Policy
     2→
     3→## Supported Versions
     4→
     5→| Platform | Version | Supported |
     6→|----------|---------|-----------|
     7→| macOS (Swift) | latest main | ✅ |
     8→| Windows (Rust) | latest main | ✅ |
     9→
    10→## Reporting a Vulnerability
    11→
    12→If you discover a security vulnerability in AudioCaptureKit, **please do not open a public issue.**
    13→
    14→Instead, please report it through [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/advisories/new):
    15→
    16→1. Go to the [Security Advisories](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/advisories) page
    17→2. Click **"Report a vulnerability"**
    18→3. Fill in the details and submit
    19→
    20→If you are unable to use GitHub's reporting, you may email [security@lll-solutions.com](mailto:security@lll-solutions.com) with the subject line "AudioCaptureKit Vulnerability Disclosure".
    21→
    22→### Response Timeline
    23→
    24→- A maintainer will **acknowledge** the report within **3 business days**
    25→- A detailed response with next steps will follow within **7 business days**
    26→- A fix or mitigation will be developed within **90 days** of the initial report, depending on severity and complexity
    27→
    28→### Disclosure Policy
    29→
    30→We follow a [coordinated vulnerability disclosure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coordinated_vulnerability_disclosure) process:
    31→
    32→- Reporters are asked to keep vulnerability details confidential until a fix is released
    33→- We will coordinate a disclosure timeline with the reporter
    34→- Security advisories will be published via [GitHub Security Advisories](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/advisories) once a fix is available
    35→- Credit will be given to reporters unless they prefer to remain anonymous
    36→
    37→### What to Include
    38→
    39→- Description of the vulnerability
    40→- Steps to reproduce
    41→- Affected platform(s) and version(s)
    42→- Potential impact (e.g., data exposure, privilege escalation)
    43→- Any suggested fix or mitigation (optional)
    44→
    45→## Security Practices
    46→
    47→This project handles sensitive audio data and follows these security practices:
    48→
    49→- **Encryption**: All captured audio is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before reaching disk. No plaintext audio is stored.
    50→- **HIPAA-aware logging**: `print()` is forbidden in production code; all logging uses `os.log` / `Logger` with appropriate privacy levels.
    51→- **Dependency scanning**: Automated via [Trivy](https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy), cargo audit, cargo deny, npm audit, and [Dependabot](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/dependabot).
    52→- **Static analysis**: SwiftLint (strict mode), Clippy (deny warnings), [CodeQL](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/code-scanning), and Trivy misconfiguration scanning.
    53→- **CI enforcement**: All security checks run on every PR and on a weekly schedule.
    54→

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Build & Test Commands
     6→
     7→### macOS (Swift 6 / SPM)
     8→```bash
     9→swift build          # Build the library
    10→swift test           # Run all tests (uses Apple Testing framework, not XCTest)
    11→```
    12→
    13→### Windows (Rust 1.75+)
    14→```bash
    15→cd windows
    16→cargo build --release
    17→cargo test           # Run all tests
    18→cargo clippy         # Lint
    19→```
    20→
    21→### Linting (macOS)
    22→SwiftLint and SwiftFormat configs live in `macOS/`. Key enforced rules:
    23→- `force_unwrap`, `force_cast`, `force_try` are **errors** — never use these
    24→- `print()` is **forbidden** in non-test code — use `os.log` or `Logger` (HIPAA compliance)
    25→- Line limit: 120 chars (warning), 200 (error)
    26→- Function body: 40 lines (warning), 80 (error)
    27→
    28→## Architecture
    29→
    30→Cross-platform audio capture library: microphone + system audio mixed into a single encrypted stereo WAV file.
    31→
    32→### Pipeline (identical on both platforms)
    33→```
    34→Mic Capture (mono) ──┐
    35→                     ├──▶ StereoMixer ──▶ EncryptedFileWriter ──▶ .wav
    36→System Capture (stereo) ┘
    37→```
    38→
    39→**Stereo mixing**: Left = mic + system_L, Right = mic + system_R.
    40→
    41→### Platform Implementations
    42→
    43→| Concern | macOS (`macOS/Sources/AudioCaptureKit/`) | Windows (`windows/`) |
    44→|---------|------------------------------------------|----------------------|
    45→| Mic | `AVFoundationMicCapture` (AVFoundation) | `WasapiMicCapture` (WASAPI) |
    46→| System audio | `CoreAudioTapCapture` (Core Audio Taps, requires macOS 14.2+) | `WasapiLoopbackCapture` (WASAPI loopback) |
    47→| Session | `CompositeCaptureSession` (protocol: `AudioCaptureSession`) | `CompositeSession<M, S>` (trait: `CaptureSession`) |
    48→| Encryption | `EncryptedFileWriter` + `CaptureEncryptor` protocol | `EncryptedFileWriter` + `Encryptor` trait |
    49→| Concurrency | Actors, `UnfairLock`, structured concurrency | `parking_lot::Mutex`, threads |
    50→
    51→### Windows Crate Structure
    52→- **`audio-capture-core`** — Platform-agnostic: models, traits, mixer, ring buffer, encryption, WAV I/O
    53→- **`audio-capture-windows`** — Windows-specific: WASAPI capture providers, device enumeration, permissions
    54→
    55→### Key Design Patterns
    56→- **State machine**: `idle → configuring → ready → capturing ↔ paused → stopping → completed/failed`
    57→- **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM streaming, chunk-per-nonce. Unencrypted WAV header, encrypted audio chunks with length prefix. No plaintext audio on disk.
    58→- **Bluetooth HFP**: Mic capture does a 500ms probe to detect actual sample rate after HFP negotiation before starting real capture.
    59→- **Protocol/trait abstraction**: Capture providers are swappable via `AudioCaptureProvider` (Swift) / `CaptureProvider` (Rust).
    60→
    61→## Code Style
    62→
    63→- UTF-8, LF line endings, 4-space indentation for Swift/Rust (see `.editorconfig`)
    64→- Swift: K&R braces, alphabetized imports, no explicit `self`, trailing commas always (see `macOS/.swiftformat`)
    65→- Swift 6 strict concurrency — all types crossing concurrency boundaries must be `Sendable`
    66→
    67→

1→# Agent Instructions
     2→
     3→This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
     4→
     5→## Quick Reference
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→bd ready              # Find available work
     9→bd show <id>          # View issue details
    10→bd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
    11→bd close <id>         # Complete work
    12→bd sync               # Sync with git
    13→```
    14→
    15→## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
    16→
    17→**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
    18→
    19→**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
    20→
    21→1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
    22→2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
    23→3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
    24→4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
    25→   ```bash
    26→   git pull --rebase
    27→   bd sync
    28→   git push
    29→   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
    30→   ```
    31→5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
    32→6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
    33→7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
    34→
    35→**CRITICAL RULES:**
    36→- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
    37→- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
    38→- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
    39→- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
    40→
    41→

1→# API Reference
     2→
     3→AudioCaptureKit provides a symmetric API across macOS (Swift) and Windows (Rust). Both platforms share the same concepts: a session manages capture, providers supply audio, and an encrypted writer handles storage.
     4→
     5→## Quick Start
     6→
     7→### macOS (Swift)
     8→
     9→```swift
    10→import AudioCaptureKit
    11→
    12→let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    13→    sampleRate: 48000,
    14→    bitDepth: 16,
    15→    channels: 2,
    16→    outputDirectory: URL.temporaryDirectory
    17→)
    18→let session = CompositeCaptureSession(configuration: config)
    19→session.delegate = myDelegate
    20→
    21→try session.configure(config)
    22→try await session.startCapture()
    23→// ...
    24→let result = try await session.stopCapture()
    25→```
    26→
    27→### Windows (Rust)
    28→
    29→```rust
    30→use audio_capture_core::{CaptureConfiguration, CompositeSession};
    31→use audio_capture_windows::{WasapiMicCapture, WasapiLoopbackCapture};
    32→
    33→let config = CaptureConfiguration {
    34→    sample_rate: 48000.0,
    35→    bit_depth: 16,
    36→    channels: 2,
    37→    output_directory: std::env::temp_dir(),
    38→    ..Default::default()
    39→};
    40→let mut session = CompositeSession::new(
    41→    WasapiMicCapture::new(),
    42→    WasapiLoopbackCapture::new(),
    43→);
    44→session.configure(config)?;
    45→session.start_capture()?;
    46→// ...
    47→let result = session.stop_capture()?;
    48→```
    49→
    50→---
    51→
    52→## Session
    53→
    54→The main entry point for audio capture.
    55→
    56→### Protocol / Trait
    57→
    58→| Swift | Rust |
    59→|-------|------|
    60→| `AudioCaptureSession` protocol | `CaptureSession` trait |
    61→
    62→### Implementation
    63→
    64→| Swift | Rust |
    65→|-------|------|
    66→| `CompositeCaptureSession` | `CompositeSession<M, S>` |
    67→
    68→### Methods
    69→
    70→| Operation | Swift | Rust |
    71→|-----------|-------|------|
    72→| Configure | `configure(_ config: CaptureConfiguration) throws` | `configure(&mut self, config: CaptureConfiguration) -> Result<()>` |
    73→| Start | `startCapture() async throws` | `start_capture(&mut self) -> Result<()>` |
    74→| Pause | `pauseCapture() throws` | `pause_capture(&mut self) -> Result<()>` |
    75→| Resume | `resumeCapture() throws` | `resume_capture(&mut self) -> Result<()>` |
    76→| Stop | `stopCapture() async throws -> RecordingResult` | `stop_capture(&mut self) -> Result<RecordingResult>` |
    77→| State | `var state: CaptureState { get }` | `fn state(&self) -> CaptureState` |
    78→| Levels | `var currentLevels: AudioLevels { get }` | `fn current_levels(&self) -> AudioLevels` |
    79→| Sources | `availableAudioSources() async throws -> [AudioSource]` | `fn available_audio_sources(&self) -> Result<Vec<AudioSource>>` |
    80→| Diagnostics | `var diagnostics: CaptureSessionDiagnostics { get }` | `fn diagnostics(&self) -> CaptureSessionDiagnostics` |
    81→
    82→---
    83→
    84→## Configuration
    85→
    86→### CaptureConfiguration
    87→
    88→| Property | Swift | Rust | Default |
    89→|----------|-------|------|---------|
    90→| Sample rate | `sampleRate: Double` | `sample_rate: f64` | 48000 |
    91→| Bit depth | `bitDepth: Int` | `bit_depth: u16` | 16 |
    92→| Channels | `channels: Int` | `channels: u16` | 2 |
    93→| Output directory | `outputDirectory: URL` | `output_directory: PathBuf` | required |
    94→| Encryptor | `encryptor: (any CaptureEncryptor)?` | `encryptor: Option<Box<dyn CaptureEncryptor>>` | nil/None |
    95→| Max duration | `maxDuration: TimeInterval?` | `max_duration_secs: Option<f64>` | nil/None |
    96→| Mic device | `micDeviceID: String?` | `mic_device_id: Option<String>` | nil/None (default device) |
    97→| Enable mic | `enableMicCapture: Bool` | `enable_mic_capture: bool` | true |
    98→| Enable system | `enableSystemCapture: Bool` | `enable_system_capture: bool` | true |
    99→
   100→Valid bit depths: 16, 24, 32.
   101→
   102→Valid channel counts: 1–4. Channels 3–4 are reserved for future multi-mic support; the mixer currently produces 2-channel output regardless.
   103→
   104→| Property | Swift | Rust | Default |
   105→|----------|-------|------|---------|
   106→| Mixing strategy | `mixingStrategy: MixingStrategy` | `mixing_strategy: MixingStrategy` | `.blended` / `Blended` |
   107→| Export raw PCM | `exportRawPCM: Bool` | `export_raw_pcm: bool` | false |
   108→
   109→See [DIARIZATION.md](DIARIZATION.md) for full mixing strategy documentation.
   110→
   111→---
   112→
   113→## State Machine
   114→
   115→Both platforms use identical state transitions:
   116→
   117→```
   118→idle → configuring → ready → capturing ↔ paused → stopping → completed/failed
   119→```
   120→
   121→### CaptureState
   122→
   123→| State | Swift | Rust |
   124→|-------|-------|------|
   125→| Idle | `.idle` | `Idle` |
   126→| Configuring | `.configuring` | `Configuring` |
   127→| Ready | `.ready` | `Ready` |
   128→| Capturing | `.capturing(duration: TimeInterval)` | `Capturing { duration_secs: f64 }` |
   129→| Paused | `.paused(duration: TimeInterval)` | `Paused { duration_secs: f64 }` |
   130→| Stopping | `.stopping` | `Stopping` |
   131→| Completed | `.completed(RecordingResult)` | `Completed(Box<RecordingResult>)` |
   132→| Failed | `.failed(CaptureError)` | `Failed(CaptureError)` |
   133→
   134→---
   135→
   136→## Delegate / Callbacks
   137→
   138→### Protocol / Trait
   139→
   140→| Swift | Rust |
   141→|-------|------|
   142→| `AudioCaptureDelegate` protocol | `CaptureDelegate` trait |
   143→
   144→### Callbacks
   145→
   146→| Event | Swift | Rust |
   147→|-------|-------|------|
   148→| State changed | `captureSession(_:didChangeState:)` | `on_state_changed(&self, state: &CaptureState)` |
   149→| Levels updated | `captureSession(_:didUpdateLevels:)` | `on_levels_updated(&self, levels: &AudioLevels)` |
   150→| Error | `captureSession(_:didEncounterError:)` | `on_error(&self, error: &CaptureError)` |
   151→| Finished | `captureSession(_:didFinishCapture:)` | `on_capture_finished(&self, result: &RecordingResult)` |
   152→| Channel buffers | `captureSession(_:didProduceChannelBuffers:)` | `set_channel_buffer_callback(cb: ChannelBufferCallback)` |
   153→
   154→The `didProduceChannelBuffers` callback (Swift) / `ChannelBufferCallback` (Rust) fires on every processing cycle (~100 ms) with raw per-channel audio before mixing. Has a default no-op implementation in Swift so existing delegates compile unchanged. See [DIARIZATION.md](DIARIZATION.md) for usage examples.
   155→
   156→---
   157→
   158→## Capture Providers
   159→
   160→Swappable audio sources that feed into the session.
   161→
   162→### Protocol / Trait
   163→
   164→| Swift | Rust |
   165→|-------|------|
   166→| `AudioCaptureProvider` protocol | `CaptureProvider` trait |
   167→
   168→### Built-in Providers
   169→
   170→| Source | Swift | Rust |
   171→|--------|-------|------|
   172→| Microphone | `AVFoundationMicCapture` | `WasapiMicCapture` |
   173→| System audio | `CoreAudioTapCapture` | `WasapiLoopbackCapture` |
   174→
   175→### Methods
   176→
   177→| Operation | Swift | Rust |
   178→|-----------|-------|------|
   179→| Check availability | `var isAvailable: Bool` | `fn is_available(&self) -> bool` |
   180→| Start | `start(bufferCallback:) async throws` | `fn start(&mut self, callback: AudioBufferCallback) -> Result<()>` |
   181→| Stop | `stop() async` | `fn stop(&mut self) -> Result<()>` |
   182→
   183→---
   184→
   185→## Encryption
   186→
   187→All encryption uses AES-256-GCM with streaming chunk-per-nonce. The WAV header is written unencrypted; audio chunks are encrypted with a length prefix.
   188→
   189→### Protocol / Trait
   190→
   191→| Swift | Rust |
   192→|-------|------|
   193→| `CaptureEncryptor` protocol | `CaptureEncryptor` trait |
   194→
   195→### Methods
   196→
   197→| Operation | Swift | Rust |
   198→|-----------|-------|------|
   199→| Encrypt | `encrypt(_ data: Data) throws -> Data` | `fn encrypt(&self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>>` |
   200→| Metadata | `keyMetadata() -> [String: String]` | `fn key_metadata(&self) -> HashMap<String, String>` |
   201→| Algorithm | `var algorithm: String` | `fn algorithm(&self) -> &str` |
   202→
   203→### EncryptedFileWriter
   204→
   205→| Operation | Swift | Rust |
   206→|-----------|-------|------|
   207→| Create | `init(fileURL:encryptor:)` | `fn new(file_path, encryptor) -> Self` |
   208→| Open | `open(configuration:) throws` | `fn open(&mut self, config) -> Result<()>` |
   209→| Write | `write(_ data: Data) throws` | `fn write(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<()>` |
   210→| Close | `close(actualSampleRate:channels:bitDepth:) throws -> String` | `fn close(&mut self, actual_sample_rate, channels, bit_depth) -> Result<String>` |
   211→| Bytes written | `var bytesWritten: UInt64` | `fn bytes_written(&self) -> u64` |
   212→
   213→---
   214→
   215→## Models
   216→
   217→### RecordingResult
   218→
   219→Returned by `stopCapture()`.
   220→
   221→| Property | Swift | Rust |
   222→|----------|-------|------|
   223→| File location | `fileURL: URL` | `file_path: PathBuf` |
   224→| Duration | `duration: TimeInterval` | `duration_secs: f64` |
   225→| Metadata | `metadata: RecordingMetadata` | `metadata: RecordingMetadata` |
   226→| Checksum | `checksum: String` | `checksum: String` |
   227→| Raw PCM files | `rawPCMFileURLs: [URL]` | `raw_pcm_file_paths: Vec<PathBuf>` |
   228→
   229→`rawPCMFileURLs` / `raw_pcm_file_paths` is empty unless `exportRawPCM` was enabled. When populated: index 0 = mic (mono), index 1 = system (stereo interleaved).
   230→
   231→### ChannelBuffers
   232→
   233→Raw per-channel audio from one processing cycle. Delivered via the channel buffers callback before mixing.
   234→
   235→| Property | Swift | Rust |
   236→|----------|-------|------|
   237→| Mic audio | `micSamples: [Float]` | `mic_samples: Vec<f32>` |
   238→| System audio | `systemSamples: [Float]` | `system_samples: Vec<f32>` |
   239→| Sample rate | `sampleRate: Double` | `sample_rate: f64` |
   240→| Timestamp | `timestamp: Date` | `timestamp_unix_secs: f64` |
   241→
   242→`systemSamples` / `system_samples` is always full interleaved stereo `[L0, R0, L1, R1, ...]`. The library never folds it to mono.
   243→
   244→### ChannelLayout
   245→
   246→Records the actual WAV channel layout in `RecordingMetadata`. Old recordings without this field decode as `blended`.
   247→
   248→| Value | Swift | Rust | Meaning |
   249→|-------|-------|------|---------|
   250→| Blended | `.blended` | `Blended` | Mic mixed into both channels |
   251→| Separated stereo | `.separatedStereo` | `SeparatedStereo` | Ch1 = mic, Ch2 = system mono-fold |
   252→| Mono | `.mono` | `Mono` | Single mono channel |
   253→
   254→### MixingStrategy
   255→
   256→| Value | Swift | Rust | WAV layout |
   257→|-------|-------|------|-----------|
   258→| Blended (default) | `.blended` | `Blended` | mic+sysL / mic+sysR |
   259→| Separated | `.separated` | `Separated` | mic only / system (L+R)/2 |
   260→| Multichannel | `.multichannel` | `Multichannel` | same as separated (reserved) |
   261→
   262→### AudioLevels
   263→
   264→Real-time audio level metering.
   265→
   266→| Property | Swift | Rust |
   267→|----------|-------|------|
   268→| Mic level | `micLevel: Float` | `mic_level: f32` |
   269→| System level | `systemLevel: Float` | `system_level: f32` |
   270→| Peak mic | `peakMicLevel: Float` | `peak_mic_level: f32` |
   271→| Peak system | `peakSystemLevel: Float` | `peak_system_level: f32` |
   272→
   273→### AudioSource
   274→
   275→Represents a discovered audio device.
   276→
   277→| Property | Swift | Rust |
   278→|----------|-------|------|
   279→| ID | `id: String` | `id: String` |
   280→| Name | `name: String` | `name: String` |
   281→| Type | `type: AudioTrackType` | `source_type: AudioTrackType` |
   282→| Default | `isDefault: Bool` | `is_default: bool` |
   283→| Transport | `transportType: AudioTransportType?` | `transport_type: Option<AudioTransportType>` |
   284→
   285→### CaptureError
   286→
   287→| Variant | Swift | Rust |
   288→|---------|-------|------|
   289→| Permission denied | `.permissionDenied` | `PermissionDenied` |
   290→| Device unavailable | `.deviceNotAvailable` | `DeviceNotAvailable` |
   291→| Config failed | `.configurationFailed(String)` | `ConfigurationFailed(String)` |
   292→| Encoding failed | `.encodingFailed(String)` | `EncodingFailed(String)` |
   293→| Encryption failed | `.encryptionFailed(String)` | `EncryptionFailed(String)` |
   294→| Storage error | `.storageError(String)` | `StorageError(String)` |
   295→| Timeout | `.timeout` | `Timeout` |
   296→| Unknown | `.unknown(String)` | `Unknown(String)` |
   297→
   298→### AudioTrack
   299→
   300→| Property | Swift | Rust | Notes |
   301→|----------|-------|------|-------|
   302→| Type | `type: AudioTrackType` | `track_type: AudioTrackType` | |
   303→| Channel | `channel: AudioChannel` | `channel: AudioChannel` | |
   304→| Label | `label: String?` | `label: Option<String>` | Omitted from JSON when nil |
   305→
   306→### Enums
   307→
   308→**AudioTrackType**: `mic`, `system`
   309→
   310→**AudioChannel**: `left` (L), `right` (R), `center` (C), `stereo` (LR)
   311→
   312→**AudioTransportType**: `builtIn`, `bluetooth`, `bluetoothLE`, `usb`, `virtual`, `unknown`
   313→
   314→**MixingStrategy**: `blended` (default), `separated`, `multichannel` — see [DIARIZATION.md](DIARIZATION.md)
   315→
   316→**ChannelLayout**: `blended` (default), `separatedStereo`, `mono`
   317→

1→# Speaker Diarization Support
     2→
     3→AudioCaptureKit enables speaker diarization without ML inference by exploiting a structural advantage: **channel identity is known at capture time**. The library exposes this via configurable channel layout and raw per-channel buffer callbacks.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→In a typical telehealth or remote-work session:
    10→
    11→| Audio Source | Identity |
    12→|---|---|
    13→| Microphone | Local speaker (always known) |
    14→| System audio | Remote participants (routed through the OS) |
    15→
    16→These two sources are captured independently and can be kept separate in the output WAV or exposed to the caller before mixing. No speaker diarization model is needed to distinguish between "who spoke" — the library already knows.
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Channel Strategy
    21→
    22→Use `MixingStrategy` to control what goes into each WAV channel:
    23→
    24→### `blended` (default)
    25→
    26→| Channel | Content |
    27→|---|---|
    28→| Left  | mic + system_L |
    29→| Right | mic + system_R |
    30→
    31→Legacy behavior. Good for playback and listening. The mic signal is blended into both channels, preserving the stereo image of system audio. Not suitable for independent per-speaker processing.
    32→
    33→### `separated`
    34→
    35→| Channel | Content |
    36→|---|---|
    37→| Left (Ch 1)  | mic only (mono mic signal) |
    38→| Right (Ch 2) | system audio, mono-folded: (system_L + system_R) / 2 |
    39→
    40→Clean channel separation. Left = local speaker, Right = remote. The mono-fold of system audio preserves content from both left and right system channels — no panned audio is discarded. Use this for speaker-attributed transcription, AI note-taking, or compliance recording where speaker identity matters.
    41→
    42→### `multichannel` (reserved)
    43→
    44→Currently behaves identically to `separated`. Reserved for future 3–4 channel multi-mic configurations.
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Diarization Scenarios
    49→
    50→### Telehealth (trivial diarization)
    51→
    52→One local speaker (mic) and one remote participant (system audio). No diarization model needed:
    53→
    54→```swift
    55→let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    56→    mixingStrategy: .separated,
    57→    outputDirectory: recordingsURL
    58→)
    59→```
    60→
    61→Process `Ch1 (Left)` independently for the local provider and `Ch2 (Right)` for the remote patient. Speaker identity is structurally guaranteed.
    62→
    63→### Couples Counseling (two-speaker diarization on one channel)
    64→
    65→Two remote participants on a video call (system audio) with one local therapist (mic). Strategy:
    66→
    67→1. Use `mixingStrategy: .separated` to isolate the mic from system audio.
    68→2. Implement `captureSession(_:didProduceChannelBuffers:)` to receive the raw system stereo buffers.
    69→3. Run a 2-speaker diarization model only on `systemSamples` — much cheaper than diarizing the full mix.
    70→
    71→### In-Person Session (full diarization required)
    72→
    73→Multiple people speaking in the same room captured by a single microphone. Standard diarization applies. Use `didProduceChannelBuffers` to receive the mic buffer in real time and feed it to your diarization pipeline.
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## `didProduceChannelBuffers` Callback
    78→
    79→AudioCaptureKit fires this delegate method on every processing cycle (~100 ms intervals), **before** mixing and file writing. This gives callers access to the highest-quality raw audio for each source.
    80→
    81→### Swift
    82→
    83→```swift
    84→class MyDelegate: AudioCaptureDelegate {
    85→    func captureSession(
    86→        _ session: any AudioCaptureSession,
    87→        didProduceChannelBuffers buffers: ChannelBuffers
    88→    ) {
    89→        // micSamples: mono Float32 array at session sample rate
    90→        let micSamples = buffers.micSamples
    91→
    92→        // systemSamples: interleaved stereo [L0, R0, L1, R1, ...]
    93→        // The library does NOT fold this to mono. If you need mono:
    94→        let systemMono = stride(from: 0, to: buffers.systemSamples.count, by: 2).map {
    95→            (buffers.systemSamples[$0] + buffers.systemSamples[$0 + 1]) / 2
    96→        }
    97→
    98→        // Feed into real-time ASR, level meters, etc.
    99→        // Non-blocking: dispatch heavy work to a background queue.
   100→        Task.detached {
   101→            await self.speechEngine.process(mic: micSamples)
   102→        }
   103→    }
   104→}
   105→```
   106→
   107→> **Important:** `systemSamples` is full interleaved stereo. If your pipeline requires mono system audio (e.g., for single-channel ASR), average the channels yourself: `(system[2i] + system[2i+1]) / 2`. The library never degrades audio quality on behalf of the caller.
   108→
   109→> **Threading:** This callback fires on an unspecified background queue. Implementations must be non-blocking. Dispatch heavy work asynchronously.
   110→
   111→### Rust
   112→
   113→```rust
   114→session.set_channel_buffer_callback(Arc::new(|buffers: &ChannelBuffers| {
   115→    // mic_samples: mono f32 slice
   116→    // system_samples: interleaved stereo [L0, R0, L1, R1, ...]
   117→    let _mic = &buffers.mic_samples;
   118→    let _system = &buffers.system_samples;
   119→    // Non-blocking: spawn a thread or send to a channel
   120→}));
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Raw PCM Sidecar Files
   126→
   127→Enable `exportRawPCM` to write unencrypted raw PCM files alongside the main WAV for use with external tools (whisper.cpp, Sox, FFmpeg, etc.):
   128→
   129→```swift
   130→let config = CaptureConfiguration(
   131→    mixingStrategy: .separated,
   132→    exportRawPCM: true,
   133→    outputDirectory: recordingsURL
   134→)
   135→```
   136→
   137→This produces two additional files:
   138→
   139→| File | Format | Content |
   140→|---|---|---|
   141→| `{name}_mic.pcm` | Signed 16-bit LE, no header, mono | Microphone channel only |
   142→| `{name}_system.pcm` | Signed 16-bit LE, no header, interleaved stereo | System audio, full stereo |
   143→
   144→The PCM sidecar files are **never encrypted**, even when the WAV file is encrypted.
   145→
   146→Their paths are available in `RecordingResult.rawPCMFileURLs` (Swift) / `RecordingResult.raw_pcm_file_paths` (Rust):
   147→- Index 0: mic PCM
   148→- Index 1: system PCM
   149→
   150→### Using with external tools
   151→
   152→```bash
   153→# Transcribe mic with whisper.cpp
   154→ffmpeg -f s16le -ar 48000 -ac 1 -i recording_mic.pcm mic.wav
   155→./whisper -m models/ggml-base.en.bin -f mic.wav
   156→
   157→# Transcribe system audio
   158→ffmpeg -f s16le -ar 48000 -ac 2 -i recording_system.pcm system.wav
   159→./whisper -m models/ggml-base.en.bin -f system.wav
   160→```
   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## `MixingStrategy` Reference
   165→
   166→| Strategy | WAV Ch1 (Left) | WAV Ch2 (Right) | Use case |
   167→|---|---|---|---|
   168→| `blended` | mic + system_L | mic + system_R | Playback, recording review |
   169→| `separated` | mic only (mono) | system mono-fold (L+R)/2 | Diarization, AI transcription |
   170→| `multichannel` | _(same as separated)_ | _(same as separated)_ | Reserved |
   171→
   172→### Swift
   173→
   174→```swift
   175→public enum MixingStrategy: Sendable, Codable {
   176→    case blended
   177→    case separated
   178→    case multichannel
   179→}
   180→```
   181→
   182→### Rust
   183→
   184→```rust
   185→pub enum MixingStrategy {
   186→    Blended,    // default
   187→    Separated,
   188→    Multichannel,
   189→}
   190→```
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## `ChannelLayout` in `RecordingMetadata`
   195→
   196→`RecordingMetadata` includes a `channelLayout` field so decoders never need to guess the WAV layout:
   197→
   198→| `MixingStrategy` | `ChannelLayout` |
   199→|---|---|
   200→| `blended` | `blended` |
   201→| `separated` | `separatedStereo` |
   202→| `multichannel` | `separatedStereo` |
   203→
   204→Old recordings without a `channelLayout` field deserialize as `blended` for backward compatibility.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## AudioTrack Labels
   209→
   210→When using `separated` strategy, `RecordingMetadata.tracks` contains labeled tracks:
   211→
   212→```json
   213→[
   214→  { "type": "mic",    "channel": "L", "label": "Mic (Local)" },
   215→  { "type": "system", "channel": "R", "label": "System (Remote, mono-fold)" }
   216→]
   217→```
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## Future: Multi-Mic (3–4 Channel)
   222→
   223→The mixer reserves `multichannel` strategy and channel counts 3–4 in `CaptureConfiguration` for future multi-mic support. The current implementation produces 2-channel output regardless of channel count. Support for a third dedicated system-stereo channel is planned.
   224→
   225→When implemented, a 3-channel layout will provide:
   226→
   227→| Channel | Content |
   228→|---|---|
   229→| Ch 1 (Left)  | mic only |
   230→| Ch 2 (Right) | system_L |
   231→| Ch 3 | system_R |
   232→
   233→This will require WAV format changes and is tracked separately.
   234→

1→# iOS Audio Capture — Design Document
     2→
     3→**Status:** Proposed
     4→**Last updated:** 2026-03-05
     5→
     6→## Background
     7→
     8→AudioCaptureKit currently targets macOS only, using Core Audio Taps for system audio and AVFoundation for microphone. This document defines the architecture for extending the library to iOS, covering in-person meeting recording and video call recording (Zoom, Teams, FaceTime, etc.).
     9→
    10→## iOS vs macOS Capabilities
    11→
    12→| Capability | macOS | iOS |
    13→|---|---|---|
    14→| Microphone capture | AVFoundation | AVFoundation (AVAudioSession) |
    15→| System audio capture | Core Audio Taps (macOS 14.2+) | **Not available** |
    16→| Video call audio | Via system tap | Via Broadcast Upload Extension |
    17→| Cellular call recording | N/A | **Not possible (Apple sandbox)** |
    18→| VoIP call recording (your app) | Full | Full via AVAudioSession + CallKit |
    19→
    20→**Key constraint:** iOS has no equivalent to `CATapDescription` / `AudioHardwareCreateProcessTap`. Third-party apps cannot capture another app's audio — with one exception: the Broadcast Upload Extension receives `.audioApp` samples for the active foreground app.
    21→
    22→## Use Cases
    23→
    24→### UC-1: In-Person Meeting Recording
    25→User opens the app, selects a patient, taps Record. The mic captures room audio. No system audio track.
    26→
    27→**Pipeline:**
    28→```
    29→Mic (AVAudioSession) → StereoMixer (mono passthrough) → EncryptedFileWriter → .wav
    30→```
    31→
    32→### UC-2: Video Call Recording (Zoom, Teams, FaceTime, WhatsApp, etc.)
    33→User selects a patient, taps "Start Recording", confirms the broadcast prompt, then joins/is in a video call. The Broadcast Upload Extension captures both audio legs.
    34→
    35→**Pipeline:**
    36→```
    37→.audioMic  (CMSampleBuffer) ──┐
    38→                               ├── BroadcastCaptureProvider → StereoMixer → EncryptedFileWriter → .wav
    39→.audioApp  (CMSampleBuffer) ──┘
    40→```
    41→- Left channel = mic (your voice)
    42→- Right channel = app audio (far end — what comes through the speaker)
    43→
    44→**User flow:**
    45→1. User selects patient in app
    46→2. App stores patient context in shared App Group (`group.com.pablo.audiocapturekit`)
    47→3. App presents `RPSystemBroadcastPickerView` — a native in-app button
    48→4. User taps "Start Broadcast" (one tap, inside your app)
    49→5. Broadcast Extension launches, reads patient context from App Group
    50→6. App deep-links to meeting: `UIApplication.shared.open(URL("zoomus://..."))`
    51→7. Recording runs in background while Zoom is foreground
    52→8. User taps Stop in status bar (or app stops programmatically via `RPBroadcastActivityViewController`)
    53→9. Main app finalizes recording, associates with patient
    54→
    55→### UC-3: Cellular Phone Calls
    56→**Not feasible.** Apple's sandbox prevents any third-party extension from accessing cellular call audio. iOS 18.1 added native call recording but exposed no API. This use case is out of scope.
    57→
    58→### UC-4: VoIP App (Future)
    59→If pablo-health ships its own calling app using CallKit, the app owns the `AVAudioSession` and can record both legs natively. Out of scope for AudioCaptureKit library itself.
    60→
    61→## Architecture
    62→
    63→### New Types (iOS only)
    64→
    65→**`IOSMicCaptureSession`** — conforms to `AudioCaptureSession`
    66→- Wraps `AVFoundationMicCapture` (reused as-is)
    67→- Handles `AVAudioSession` lifecycle: `.record` category, interruption notifications (phone calls, Siri)
    68→- `enableSystemCapture` config flag is silently ignored on iOS
    69→- Platform: iOS 16+
    70→
    71→**`BroadcastCaptureProvider`** — conforms to `AudioCaptureProvider`
    72→- Lives in a separate App Extension target (not in the main app)
    73→- Subclass of `RPBroadcastSampleHandler`
    74→- Converts `CMSampleBuffer` (`.audioMic`, `.audioApp`) → `AVAudioPCMBuffer`
    75→- Writes to a shared `AudioBufferManager` via App Group file or IPC
    76→- Platform: iOS 16+
    77→
    78→### Reused (unchanged)
    79→- `StereoMixer` — already platform-agnostic
    80→- `EncryptedFileWriter` — already platform-agnostic (actor)
    81→- `AudioBufferManager` — already platform-agnostic
    82→- `CaptureConfiguration` — add iOS-specific validation
    83→- `RecordingResult`, `RecordingMetadata`, `AudioLevels` — unchanged
    84→
    85→### Package Structure
    86→```
    87→Package.swift — add .iOS(.v16) to platforms
    88→macOS/Sources/AudioCaptureKit/   — unchanged
    89→iOS/Sources/AudioCaptureKit/
    90→  Capture/
    91→    IOSMicCaptureSession.swift
    92→    BroadcastCaptureProvider.swift
    93→  Shared/                        — symlinks to cross-platform processing code
    94→```
    95→
    96→### App Group IPC
    97→The Broadcast Extension runs in a separate process. Communication with the main app uses a shared App Group container:
    98→- `UserDefaults(suiteName: "group.com.pablo.audiocapturekit")` for patient context
    99→- Shared file path for audio buffer hand-off on extension stop
   100→
   101→## Entitlements Required
   102→- `com.apple.security.application-groups` — for App Group shared container
   103→- `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription` — mic access
   104→- No special entitlements needed for Broadcast Upload Extension (ReplayKit is public API)
   105→
   106→## What This Does NOT Support
   107→- Cellular call recording (Apple sandbox; impossible)
   108→- System-wide audio capture (no iOS equivalent to Core Audio Taps)
   109→- Silent/background recording without user initiating broadcast
   110→- Zoom raw PCM without screen recording (would require backend bot infrastructure)
   111→
   112→## Open Questions
   113→- Should `BroadcastCaptureProvider` be a separate Swift package target, or bundled as an extension template?
   114→- Encryption key management across app ↔ extension boundary (Keychain with shared access group)
   115→- Max recording duration in extension context (iOS may terminate long-running extensions)
   116→

1→# Android Audio Capture — Design Document
     2→
     3→**Status:** Proposed
     4→**Last updated:** 2026-03-05
     5→
     6→## Background
     7→
     8→AudioCaptureKit currently targets macOS (Swift/AVFoundation + Core Audio Taps) and Windows (Rust/WASAPI). This document defines the architecture for extending the library to Android, where — unlike iOS — a real system audio capture API exists: `AudioPlaybackCapture` via `MediaProjection` (Android 10 / API 29+). This gives Android feature parity with macOS and Windows for the full mic + system audio pipeline.
     9→
    10→## Android vs macOS Capabilities
    11→
    12→| Capability | macOS | Android |
    13→|---|---|---|
    14→| Microphone capture | AVFoundation | AudioRecord (API 1+) ✓ |
    15→| System audio capture | Core Audio Taps (macOS 14.2+) | AudioPlaybackCapture + MediaProjection (API 29+) ✓ |
    16→| Cellular call audio | N/A | Not possible — VOICE_COMMUNICATION excluded from AudioPlaybackCapture |
    17→| Concurrency model | Actors, structured concurrency | Kotlin coroutines |
    18→
    19→**Key advantage over iOS:** Android's `AudioPlaybackCapture` API allows third-party apps to capture system audio (with user consent via `MediaProjection`), enabling the same full stereo pipeline as macOS and Windows. Apps can opt out via `android:allowAudioPlaybackCapture="false"` in their manifest.
    20→
    21→## Use Cases
    22→
    23→### UC-1: In-Person Meeting Recording
    24→User opens the app, selects a patient, taps Record. The mic captures room audio. No system audio needed.
    25→
    26→**Pipeline:**
    27→```
    28→Mic (AudioRecord) → StereoMixer (mono passthrough) → EncryptedFileWriter → .wav
    29→```
    30→
    31→**Minimum API:** 23 (Android 6.0 — runtime permissions required for RECORD_AUDIO)
    32→
    33→### UC-2: Screen/App Audio Recording
    34→User selects a patient, taps "Start Recording", grants MediaProjection consent, then joins a video call. Both mic and system audio are captured and mixed.
    35→
    36→**Pipeline:**
    37→```
    38→Mic (AudioRecord) ──────────────────────────────────────┐
    39→                                                         ├──▶ StereoMixer ──▶ EncryptedFileWriter ──▶ .wav
    40→System audio (AudioRecord + AudioPlaybackCaptureConfig) ─┘
    41→```
    42→
    43→- Left channel = mic (your voice) + system_L
    44→- Right channel = mic (your voice) + system_R
    45→
    46→**User flow:**
    47→1. User selects patient in app
    48→2. App requests MediaProjection via `MediaProjectionManager.createScreenCaptureIntent()`
    49→3. User approves consent dialog (one tap)
    50→4. App stores projection token, starts foreground service
    51→5. `AndroidPlaybackCaptureProvider` creates `AudioRecord` with `AudioPlaybackCaptureConfiguration`
    52→6. `AndroidMicCaptureProvider` starts mic `AudioRecord` in parallel
    53→7. Both PCM streams feed into `StereoMixer`
    54→8. User taps Stop; foreground service tears down both capture providers
    55→
    56→**Minimum API:** 29 (Android 10)
    57→
    58→### UC-3: Cellular Phone Calls
    59→**Not feasible.** Android excludes `USAGE_VOICE_COMMUNICATION` from `AudioPlaybackCapture` by design. No public API grants access to cellular call audio for third-party apps.
    60→
    61→## Architecture — New Types
    62→
    63→**`AndroidMicCaptureProvider`** — conforms to `CaptureProvider`-equivalent interface
    64→- Wraps `android.media.AudioRecord` with `AudioFormat.CHANNEL_IN_MONO`, `AudioFormat.ENCODING_PCM_16BIT`
    65→- Runs on a dedicated coroutine reading PCM buffers in a loop
    66→- Handles `RECORD_AUDIO` permission check before initialization
    67→- Reports actual sample rate after construction (device-dependent)
    68→- Platform: API 23+
    69→
    70→**`AndroidPlaybackCaptureProvider`** — conforms to `CaptureProvider`-equivalent interface
    71→- Creates `AudioRecord` via `AudioRecord.Builder` with `AudioPlaybackCaptureConfiguration`
    72→- Requires a valid `MediaProjection` token passed in at construction
    73→- Captures stereo PCM (system default sample rate, typically 44100 or 48000 Hz)
    74→- Releases `MediaProjection` on stop
    75→- Platform: API 29+
    76→
    77→**`AndroidCaptureSession`** — state machine + foreground service lifecycle
    78→- State machine: `idle → configuring → ready → capturing ↔ paused → stopping → completed/failed`
    79→- Runs as a bound `ForegroundService` with `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_MICROPHONE` (API 29+) or `FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_MEDIA_PROJECTION` (API 34+)
    80→- Manages both providers via coroutine scopes
    81→- Exposes `Flow<AudioLevels>` and `Flow<CaptureState>` for UI observation
    82→- On pause: stops reading PCM buffers but keeps `AudioRecord` instances allocated
    83→- On stop: finalizes `EncryptedFileWriter`, releases all resources
    84→
    85→## Reused Components
    86→
    87→| Component | Reuse strategy |
    88→|---|---|
    89→| `StereoMixer` | Port math to Kotlin (identical algorithm: L = mic + sys_L, R = mic + sys_R) |
    90→| `EncryptedFileWriter` | Re-implement in Kotlin using Conscrypt or `javax.crypto` AES-256-GCM |
    91→| WAV format | Port WAV header writer from Rust (`audio-capture-core`) to Kotlin |
    92→| State machine | Replicate `idle → capturing → completed/failed` transitions in `AndroidCaptureSession` |
    93→
    94→## Language & Build
    95→
    96→- **Language:** Kotlin (coroutines for async, `Flow` for streaming state)
    97→- **Directory:** `android/` at repo root
    98→- **Build system:** Gradle (Kotlin DSL), `android/build.gradle.kts`
    99→- **Min SDK:** 23 for mic-only; 29 for full pipeline
   100→- **Target SDK:** latest stable (API 35)
   101→
   102→## Permissions Required
   103→
   104→```xml
   105→<!-- AndroidManifest.xml -->
   106→<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" />
   107→<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE" />
   108→<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MICROPHONE" />
   109→<!-- API 34+ requires explicit media projection foreground service type -->
   110→<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION" />
   111→```
   112→
   113→`MediaProjection` does not require a manifest permission — it requires a runtime user consent dialog via `MediaProjectionManager`.
   114→
   115→## Known Limitations
   116→
   117→- **App opt-out:** Apps with `android:allowAudioPlaybackCapture="false"` in their manifest are excluded from system capture. This includes some banking and DRM-protected media apps.
   118→- **OEM variations:** Some OEM audio stacks report different sample rates or channel configurations than AOSP. `AndroidMicCaptureProvider` must query the actual sample rate post-construction.
   119→- **No telephony audio:** `USAGE_VOICE_COMMUNICATION` (phone calls, VoIP) is excluded from `AudioPlaybackCapture` by the OS.
   120→- **Foreground service requirement:** Android 14 (API 34) enforces explicit `foregroundServiceType` for MediaProjection services and requires the user to re-grant consent if the app is killed.
   121→
   122→## Open Questions
   123→
   124→- **Min API strategy:** Ship mic-only path at API 23 and system audio path at API 29? Or require API 29 for the library and let the host app handle the fallback UI?
   125→- **Encryption provider:** `javax.crypto` (built-in, no extra dependency) vs. Conscrypt (Google's BoringSSL-based provider, stronger AES-GCM guarantees) vs. Signal's `AES-GCM-Provider`?
   126→- **Sample app:** Standalone Android app in `android/sample/`, or a Compose Multiplatform example shared with a future KMP integration?
   127→

1→# Beads - AI-Native Issue Tracking
     2→
     3→Welcome to Beads! This repository uses **Beads** for issue tracking - a modern, AI-native tool designed to live directly in your codebase alongside your code.
     4→
     5→## What is Beads?
     6→
     7→Beads is issue tracking that lives in your repo, making it perfect for AI coding agents and developers who want their issues close to their code. No web UI required - everything works through the CLI and integrates seamlessly with git.
     8→
     9→**Learn more:** [github.com/steveyegge/beads](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads)
    10→
    11→## Quick Start
    12→
    13→### Essential Commands
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→# Create new issues
    17→bd create "Add user authentication"
    18→
    19→# View all issues
    20→bd list
    21→
    22→# View issue details
    23→bd show <issue-id>
    24→
    25→# Update issue status
    26→bd update <issue-id> --status in_progress
    27→bd update <issue-id> --status done
    28→
    29→# Sync with git remote
    30→bd sync
    31→```
    32→
    33→### Working with Issues
    34→
    35→Issues in Beads are:
    36→- **Git-native**: Stored in `.beads/issues.jsonl` and synced like code
    37→- **AI-friendly**: CLI-first design works perfectly with AI coding agents
    38→- **Branch-aware**: Issues can follow your branch workflow
    39→- **Always in sync**: Auto-syncs with your commits
    40→
    41→## Why Beads?
    42→
    43→✨ **AI-Native Design**
    44→- Built specifically for AI-assisted development workflows
    45→- CLI-first interface works seamlessly with AI coding agents
    46→- No context switching to web UIs
    47→
    48→🚀 **Developer Focused**
    49→- Issues live in your repo, right next to your code
    50→- Works offline, syncs when you push
    51→- Fast, lightweight, and stays out of your way
    52→
    53→🔧 **Git Integration**
    54→- Automatic sync with git commits
    55→- Branch-aware issue tracking
    56→- Intelligent JSONL merge resolution
    57→
    58→## Get Started with Beads
    59→
    60→Try Beads in your own projects:
    61→
    62→```bash
    63→# Install Beads
    64→curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
    65→
    66→# Initialize in your repo
    67→bd init
    68→
    69→# Create your first issue
    70→bd create "Try out Beads"
    71→```
    72→
    73→## Learn More
    74→
    75→- **Documentation**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/docs](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/docs)
    76→- **Quick Start Guide**: Run `bd quickstart`
    77→- **Examples**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/examples](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/examples)
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→*Beads: Issue tracking that moves at the speed of thought* ⚡
    82→

     2→
     3→[![OpenSSF Scorecard](https://api.securityscorecards.dev/projects/github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/badge)](https://scorecard.dev/viewer/?uri=github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit)
     4→[![OpenSSF Best Practices](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007/badge)](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007)
     5→
     6→Cross-platform audio capture library — microphone + system audio mixed into a single stereo recording with AES-256-GCM encryption.
     7→
     8→## Platform Support
     9→
    10→| Platform | Language | Audio API | Directory |
    11→|----------|----------|-----------|-----------|
    12→| macOS 14+ | Swift 6 | Core Audio Taps + AVFoundation | `macOS/` |
    13→| Windows 10+ | Rust 1.75+ | WASAPI | `windows/` |
    14→
    15→## Shared Architecture
    16→
    17→Both platforms follow the same **Capture → Processing → Storage** pipeline:
    18→
    19→```
    20→┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
    21→│  Mic Capture     │────▶│              │     │                  │
    22→│  (mono)          │     │  StereoMixer │────▶│  EncryptedFile   │
    23→│                  │     │              │     │  Writer (.wav)   │
    24→│  System Capture  │────▶│              │     │                  │
    25→│  (stereo)        │     └──────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
    26→└─────────────────┘
    27→```
    28→
    29→**Stereo mixing** is identical on both platforms: Left = mic + system L, Right = mic + system R. Mic audio is mono, mixed into center of the stereo field. System audio preserves its natural stereo image.
    30→
    31→**Encryption**: Both platforms use AES-256-GCM to stream-encrypt audio chunks as they're captured — no plaintext on disk.
    32→
    33→## macOS
    34→
    35→### Requirements
    36→
    37→- macOS 14.0+ (system audio capture requires macOS 14.2+)
    38→- Swift 6.0+
    39→- Entitlement: `com.apple.security.device.audio-input`
    40→- TCC permission: "Screen & System Audio Recording" (for system audio)
    41→
    42→### Installation (Swift Package Manager)
    43→
    44→```swift
    45→dependencies: [
    46→    .package(path: "../AudioCaptureKit")  // local
    47→    // or
    48→    // .package(url: "https://github.com/yourorg/AudioCaptureKit.git", from: "1.0.0")
    49→]
    50→```
    51→
    52→### Quick Start
    53→
    54→```swift
    55→import AudioCaptureKit
    56→
    57→let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    58→    sampleRate: 48000,
    59→    bitDepth: 16,
    60→    channels: 2,
    61→    outputDirectory: URL.temporaryDirectory
    62→)
    63→let session = CompositeCaptureSession(configuration: config)
    64→session.delegate = myDelegate
    65→
    66→try session.configure(config)
    67→try await session.startCapture()
    68→
    69→let result = try await session.stopCapture()
    70→print("Recorded \(result.duration)s to \(result.fileURL)")
    71→```
    72→
    73→### Testing
    74→
    75→```bash
    76→swift test
    77→```
    78→
    79→See `macOS/` for source code and `Examples/macOS/` for the sample app.
    80→
    81→## Windows
    82→
    83→### Requirements
    84→
    85→- Windows 10+
    86→- Rust 1.75+
    87→
    88→### Building
    89→
    90→```bash
    91→cd windows
    92→cargo build --release
    93→```
    94→
    95→### Crate Structure
    96→
    97→| Crate | Purpose |
    98→|-------|---------|
    99→| `audio-capture-core` | Platform-agnostic models, traits, stereo mixer, encrypted writer |
   100→| `audio-capture-windows` | WASAPI mic capture, WASAPI loopback (system audio), device enumeration |
   101→
   102→### Quick Start
   103→
   104→```rust
   105→use audio_capture_core::session::CompositeSession;
   106→use audio_capture_core::models::CaptureConfig;
   107→use audio_capture_windows::{WasapiMic, WasapiLoopback};
   108→
   109→let config = CaptureConfig::new(48000, 16, 2);
   110→let session = CompositeSession::new(config);
   111→
   112→session.start()?;
   113→// ...
   114→let result = session.stop()?;
   115→```
   116→
   117→See `windows/` for source code.
   118→
   119→### Sample App (Tauri v2)
   120→
   121→A desktop GUI at `Examples/windows/SampleApp/` built with Tauri v2 (Rust backend + React frontend). Records mic + system audio, shows real-time level meters, lists recordings, and supports device selection and encryption toggle.
   122→
   123→**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Rust 1.77+, Windows 10 SDK
   124→
   125→```bash
   126→cd Examples/windows/SampleApp
   127→npm install
   128→npm run tauri dev      # Dev mode with hot reload
   129→npm run tauri build    # Produces .exe + MSI installer in src-tauri/target/release/bundle/
   130→```

file.content:
# AudioCaptureKit

[![OpenSSF Scorecard](https://api.securityscorecards.dev/projects/github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/badge)](https://scorecard.dev/viewer/?uri=github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit)
[![OpenSSF Best Practices](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007/badge)](https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/12007)

Cross-platform audio capture library — microphone + system audio mixed into a single stereo recording with AES-256-GCM encryption.

## Platform Support

| Platform | Language | Audio API | Directory |
|----------|----------|-----------|-----------|
| macOS 14+ | Swift 6 | Core Audio Taps + AVFoundation | `macOS/` |
| Windows 10+ | Rust 1.75+ | WASAPI | `windows/` |

## Shared Architecture

Both platforms follow the same **Capture → Processing → Storage** pipeline:

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
│  Mic Capture     │────▶│              │     │                  │
│  (mono)          │     │  StereoMixer │────▶│  EncryptedFile   │
│                  │     │              │     │  Writer (.wav)   │
│  System Capture  │────▶│              │     │                  │
│  (stereo)        │     └──────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
└─────────────────┘
```

**Stereo mixing** is identical on both platforms: Left = mic + system L, Right = mic + system R. Mic audio is mono, mixed into center of the stereo field. System audio preserves its natural stereo image.

**Encryption**: Both platforms use AES-256-GCM to stream-encrypt audio chunks as they're captured — no plaintext on disk.

## macOS

### Requirements

- macOS 14.0+ (system audio capture requires macOS 14.2+)
- Swift 6.0+
- Entitlement: `com.apple.security.device.audio-input`
- TCC permission: "Screen & System Audio Recording" (for system audio)

### Installation (Swift Package Manager)

```swift
dependencies: [
    .package(path: "../AudioCaptureKit")  // local
    // or
    // .package(url: "https://github.com/yourorg/AudioCaptureKit.git", from: "1.0.0")
]
```

### Quick Start

```swift
import AudioCaptureKit

let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    sampleRate: 48000,
    bitDepth: 16,
    channels: 2,
    outputDirectory: URL.temporaryDirectory
)
let session = CompositeCaptureSession(configuration: config)
session.delegate = myDelegate

try session.configure(config)
try await session.startCapture()

let result = try await session.stopCapture()
print("Recorded \(result.duration)s to \(result.fileURL)")
```

### Testing

```bash
swift test
```

See `macOS/` for source code and `Examples/macOS/` for the sample app.

## Windows

### Requirements

- Windows 10+
- Rust 1.75+

### Building

```bash
cd windows
cargo build --release
```

### Crate Structure

| Crate | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `audio-capture-core` | Platform-agnostic models, traits, stereo mixer, encrypted writer |
| `audio-capture-windows` | WASAPI mic capture, WASAPI loopback (system audio), device enumeration |

### Quick Start

```rust
use audio_capture_core::session::CompositeSession;
use audio_capture_core::models::CaptureConfig;
use audio_capture_windows::{WasapiMic, WasapiLoopback};

let config = CaptureConfig::new(48000, 16, 2);
let session = CompositeSession::new(config);

session.start()?;
// ...
let result = session.stop()?;
```

See `windows/` for source code.

### Sample App (Tauri v2)

A desktop GUI at `Examples/windows/SampleApp/` built with Tauri v2 (Rust backend + React frontend). Records mic + system audio, shows real-time level meters, lists recordings, and supports device selection and encryption toggle.

**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Rust 1.77+, Windows 10 SDK

```bash
cd Examples/windows/SampleApp
npm install
npm run tauri dev      # Dev mode with hot reload
npm run tauri build    # Produces .exe + MSI installer in src-tauri/target/release/bundle/
```

## License

This project is licensed under the [GNU Affero General Public License v3.0](LICENSE).

For commercial licensing options, contact [kurtn@lll-solutions.com](mailto:kurtn@lll-solutions.com).

1→# Security Policy
     2→
     3→## Supported Versions
     4→
     5→| Platform | Version | Supported |
     6→|----------|---------|-----------|
     7→| macOS (Swift) | latest main | ✅ |
     8→| Windows (Rust) | latest main | ✅ |
     9→
    10→## Reporting a Vulnerability
    11→
    12→If you discover a security vulnerability in AudioCaptureKit, **please do not open a public issue.**
    13→
    14→Instead, please report it through [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/advisories/new):
    15→
    16→1. Go to the [Security Advisories](https://github.com/pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit/security/advisories) page
    17→2. Click **"Report a vulnerability"**
    18→3. Fill in the details and submit
    19→
    20→If you are unable to use GitHub's reporting, you may email [security@lll-solutions.com](mailto:security@lll-solutions.com) with the subject line "AudioCaptureKit Vulnerability Disclosure".
    21→
    22→### Response Timeline
    23→
    24→- A maintainer will **acknowledge** the report within **3 business days**
    25→- A detailed response with next steps will follow within **7 business days**
